content:
# INVARIANT_MEMPOOL_NO_DUPLICATE_TX_V0

Blockchain Mempool No-Duplicate-Transaction Invariant (runtime-enforced)

## Machine

```yaml
invariant_code: INVARIANT_MEMPOOL_NO_DUPLICATE_TX_V0
artifact_kind: INVARIANT
version: V0
governed_by: fb.topology::INVARIANT_RUNTIME_INVARIANT_WIRED_V0

core:
  description: >
    No duplicate transaction may be admitted to the blockchain mempool dedup
    index. A submission whose transaction identity already exists in the mempool
    index is rejected as a business violation rather than written twice. This is
    a mempool-scoped admission rule; it is distinct from the canonical
    TRANSACTION store and does not assert anything about confirmed transactions.

  enforcement_stage:
    - runtime_outcome

  scope:
    - BLOCKCHAIN

  subdomain: transaction

  violation_response: BUSINESS_VIOLATION

  enforced_by:
    - blockchain::CC_WRITE_MEMPOOL_TX_V0

  runtime_binding:
    enforced_by: blockchain::CC_WRITE_MEMPOOL_TX_V0
    enforcing_workflow: blockchain::WF_SUBMIT_TRANSACTION_V0
    violation_outcome: ALREADY_EXISTS
    terminal_node: EXIT_DUPLICATE
    over_store: blockchain::MEMPOOL_INDEX

  anti_patterns:
    - duplicate_admission: "same transaction written to the mempool index more than once"
```

## Summary

Declares the runtime business invariant that the mempool admits each transaction
at most once. Enforcement is the existing capability-contract outcome routing:
`CC_WRITE_MEMPOOL_TX_V0` emits `ALREADY_EXISTS`, which `WF_SUBMIT_TRANSACTION_V0`
routes to the `EXIT_DUPLICATE` terminal (classified `BUSINESS_VIOLATION` in the
trace). The binding is verified at compile time by
`fb.topology::ASSERT_RUNTIME_INVARIANT_WIRED_V0`. The runtime is unchanged.

## Version History

- **V0**: Initial mempool no-duplicate-transaction invariant (2026-06-14)

anti_patterns.duplicate_admission: same transaction written to the mempool index more than once

core.description: No duplicate transaction may be admitted to the blockchain mempool dedup index. A submission whose transaction identity already exists in the mempool index is rejected as a business violation rather than written twice. This is a mempool-scoped admission rule; it is distinct from the canonical TRANSACTION store and does not assert anything about confirmed transactions.
